Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 117. Merchants (ABL 0234)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL] EN-ia

(1) [To the king], my lord: [your servant Ašš]ur-bani. [Good health to] the king, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD-ka maš]-⸢šur⸣—ba-ni

o 33

[lu DI-mu a-na] LUGAL EN-ia

(3) [As to ...... the me]rchants [......

o 44

[ina UGU mx x x]-a-ti

o 55

[x x x x x LÚv].⸢DAM⸣.QAR-MEŠ

o 66

[x x x x x x x x x]-a

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x x x] ⸢URU*.kal*-ḫa*⸣

(r 1) Calah [...... in] my hand [...... the king, my lo]rd will kill me.

r 2'2'

[x x x x x ina] qa-ti-ia

r 3'3'

[x x x LUGAL be]-lí i-du-ka-an-ni

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334173/.
